title: My Mother, The Car

jokes.text: I enjoy bicentennials as much as the next guy, Mom, but we're not doing that.
jokes.setup: Lucille wants to throw the most lavish birthday party ever
jokes.punchline: Michael treats it like it's a 200-year celebration instead of her birthday
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan misunderstanding that reveals Michael's exasperation. The word 'bicentennial' is so specific and absurd in this context, and delivered with Michael's characteristic dry precision.

jokes.text: Buster had been avoiding Lucille Austero since he accidentally flirted with her while not wearing his glasses. And then accidentally bid on her at a charity bachelorette auction. And then accidentally entered her apartment, thinking it was his mother's.
jokes.setup: Narrator explains why Buster is avoiding Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Three escalating 'accidents' that clearly weren't accidents
jokes.explanation: Classic AD escalation structure with perfect character specificity. The repeated 'accidentally' becomes funnier with each implausible scenario.

jokes.text: Ten thousand dollars! Buster.
jokes.setup: Flashback to the charity auction
jokes.punchline: Buster's shocked reaction to his own astronomical bid
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy gold - Buster's panicked realization paired with the absurd amount makes this land perfectly.

jokes.text: Oh, here comes the 10:15 conniption, right on time.
jokes.setup: Buster starts panicking
jokes.punchline: Lucille treats his panic attacks like scheduled appointments
jokes.explanation: Pure Lucille - clinical precision applied to maternal neglect. The specific time makes it even more coldly funny.

jokes.text: I have no idea where you get that from. Get away from that stove. You're going to light your hair on fire.
jokes.setup: Lucille claims ignorance about Buster's anxiety
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates the exact paranoid behavior that causes it
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure - the punchline immediately contradicts the setup, revealing Lucille's complete lack of self-awareness.

jokes.text: Protein bars for your father. He's pumping up in prison.
jokes.setup: Michael asks what's in the heavy bag
jokes.punchline: George Sr. is apparently bodybuilding in prison
jokes.explanation: Absurd image of George Sr. getting swole in prison, delivered with Lucille's matter-of-fact tone. Sets up the later Krugerrand reveal.

jokes.text: You're my third least-favorite child. I can live with that.
jokes.setup: Lucille threatens Michael with a ranking
jokes.punchline: Michael accepts third-worst as reasonable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Lucille's casual cruelty met by Michael's resigned pragmatism. The specificity of 'third least-favorite' is perfect.

jokes.text: Michael came home to find his mother's fourth least-favorite child.
jokes.setup: Previous ranking joke
jokes.punchline: Narrator immediately establishes GOB's position in the hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Immediate callback that extends the ranking system. The narrator's clinical tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Well, they got the Asian right. 'Hotties' might be a stretch.
jokes.setup: GOB heard about yacht buyers being described as 'Asian hotties'
jokes.punchline: Admits one descriptor is accurate while dismissing the other
jokes.explanation: GOB's shallow assessment reveals his character while being unexpectedly honest about his judgment.

jokes.text: Well, let's start with the little one.
jokes.setup: GOB commenting on potential yacht buyers
jokes.punchline: Implies he's ranking women like he's shopping
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent sleaziness from GOB, but not particularly clever or surprising.

jokes.text: I know, Michael. That's why this is the perfect opportunity to throw her a surprise party. She'd never suspect it.
jokes.setup: Michael just told his mother no more parties
jokes.punchline: GOB thinks this makes it the perfect time for a party
jokes.explanation: GOB's twisted logic is perfectly in character - he sees Michael's prohibition as providing cover for his scheme.

jokes.text: Michael, do you have any idea what would happen if I were to visit prison? It would be pandemonium.
jokes.setup: Michael suggests Lindsay visit their father
jokes.punchline: Lindsay thinks her presence would cause chaos due to her looks
jokes.explanation: Lindsay's narcissistic delusion is perfectly captured. Sets up the prison scenes where she's completely ignored.

jokes.text: Yeah, I got some of that... except he also didn't like my looks.
jokes.setup: Lindsay complains their father only values her looks
jokes.punchline: Michael reveals he got neither praise for looks nor anything else
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - dry, self-aware, and revealing the family's complete emotional dysfunction.

jokes.text: Come on. That would never happen.
jokes.setup: Lindsay suggests Michael would take their mother to dinner
jokes.punchline: GOB finds this completely implausible
jokes.explanation: Reveals how unusual kindness is in the Bluth family that even basic gestures seem impossible.

jokes.text: Lindsay was not exaggerating. Lucille had recently been featured on Fox's World's Worst Drivers.
jokes.setup: Lindsay claims their mother is the world's worst driver
jokes.punchline: She literally appeared on a TV show about terrible drivers
jokes.explanation: Takes Lindsay's hyperbole and makes it literally true. The Fox branding adds specificity that makes it funnier.

jokes.text: She didn't. I dummied her up a new one. Not my best work though. She wanted to look 48. I nearly airbrushed her into oblivion. We ended up checking 'Albino' on the form.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Lucille's driving
jokes.punchline: GOB made a fake license with increasingly absurd details
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from vanity to complete absurdity. 'Nearly airbrushed her into oblivion' is beautifully specific, and the albino conclusion is perfectly insane.

jokes.text: For Gangy? Yeah, how did you know? She called and invited us.
jokes.setup: Michael agrees to surprise party
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's not a surprise because Lucille invited them herself
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection that deflates Michael's plan and reveals Lucille's manipulation in one quick exchange.

jokes.text: Come on. This is a Bluth family celebration. It's no place for children.
jokes.setup: George Michael asks if they have to go to the party
jokes.punchline: Michael warns him away from his own family's gathering
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about family toxicity delivered as protective parental advice. Perfectly captures Michael's awareness of his family's dysfunction.

jokes.text: But didn't get the reception she was expecting.
jokes.setup: Lindsay visits prison for the first time
jokes.punchline: Sets up that her expectation of causing 'pandemonium' won't happen
jokes.explanation: Narrator setup that calls back to Lindsay's delusion about causing chaos with her beauty.

jokes.text: High-fastening pants.
jokes.setup: George Sr. lists what each child is known for
jokes.punchline: Buster's defining characteristic is his pants height
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and absurd character detail. The formal phrasing makes it even funnier, and it's perfectly Buster.

jokes.text: Lindsay was surprised once again that no heads were turned.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's belief she causes pandemonium
jokes.punchline: Complete indifference from other prisoners
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Lindsay's narcissistic expectations. The visual of complete disinterest is the perfect deflation.

jokes.text: It was a love between two cousins that the world thought was wrong. But it was the world that was wrong.
jokes.setup: George Michael moving beyond his crush on Maeby
jokes.punchline: Narrator frames it like a forbidden romance that should be accepted
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy that takes innocent cousin friendship and frames it as society-defying romance. The earnest narrator voice makes it even more absurd.

jokes.text: It's better than sitting here with Buster's stupid rock behind me.
jokes.setup: Lucille offers to drive
jokes.punchline: Reveals random archaeological rock is just sitting in the car
jokes.explanation: Sets up the rock detail that becomes important later. Casual mention of absurd situation is classic AD.

jokes.text: Buster had recently brought the rock home from an archeological dig, but had yet to find a way to get it out of the car.
jokes.setup: Reference to Buster's rock
jokes.punchline: He somehow got it in but can't get it out
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster logic - academic achievement that creates practical problems. The absurdity of the logistics makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You strike one more person, and it's technically a spree.
jokes.setup: Lucille has hit two people with her car
jokes.punchline: One more hit makes it a crime spree by definition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that treats vehicular violence like collecting baseball cards. The word 'technically' makes it even more absurdly clinical.

jokes.text: And she was surprised that her family didn't show up.
jokes.setup: Second 'surprise' party
jokes.punchline: Empty restaurant showing family's complete disinterest
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of the empty party reinforces the family's dysfunction. The surprised reaction adds to the pathos.

jokes.text: So this is what my children think of me.
jokes.setup: Empty surprise party
jokes.punchline: Lucille's rare moment of self-awareness about her relationships
jokes.explanation: Uncharacteristic vulnerability from Lucille creates uncomfortable comedy. The moment of clarity is both sad and funny.

jokes.text: In fact, Buster was worried about running into Lucille 2. So he never left his mother's apartment. Lucille 2 was also supposed to go, but was waiting until she saw Buster emerge.
jokes.setup: Explanation for why Buster missed the party
jokes.punchline: Both Buster and Lucille 2 are trapped by trying to avoid each other
jokes.explanation: Perfect sitcom irony - both characters' avoidance strategies cancel each other out. Classic setup for future plot developments.

jokes.text: Well, it was kind of funny.
jokes.setup: Describing Lucille's emotional journey at the party
jokes.punchline: Michael admits her devastation was amusing
jokes.explanation: Michael's honest admission of finding his mother's pain amusing reveals the family's dark dynamics.

jokes.text: I've been a horrible mother.
jokes.setup: Michael suggests she should be nicer
jokes.punchline: Lucille actually admits fault for once
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Lucille self-awareness that's immediately undermined by Michael's automatic contradiction.

jokes.text: Um, is it okay if I do it for Mom and not you?
jokes.setup: Michael asks GOB to help with the party
jokes.punchline: GOB wants to clarify his motivation excludes Michael
jokes.explanation: Perfect GOB - unnecessarily specific about his dislike for Michael while being helpful. The politeness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I've already got a Lucille in my life.
jokes.setup: Buster trying to break up with Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Treats his mother and romantic interest as interchangeable Lucilles
jokes.explanation: Brilliant encapsulation of Buster's psychological issues. The clinical tone about his mother complex is perfectly Buster.

jokes.text: Yes. I'm foolish and I'm funny and I'm needy. Am I needy? Are you sure I'm not needy? 'Cause I feel needy sometimes.
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 feeling embarrassed
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates the exact neediness she's asking about
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-defeating behavior - asking repeatedly if she's needy while being intensely needy. The escalation makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You and I. Is it 'I' or is it 'me'?
jokes.setup: Emotional conversation about their connection
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by grammar mid-sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Lucille 2's tendency to overthink everything, even romantic declarations.

jokes.text: Buster, it's us. Yes. It is us. Us.
jokes.setup: Grammar confusion during emotional moment
jokes.punchline: Both get caught up in the word choice instead of the emotion
jokes.explanation: Both characters getting caught up in linguistics during what should be an emotional breakthrough is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: I hate to tell you, Mom, but they don't support me either.
jokes.setup: Lucille complaining about lack of family support
jokes.punchline: Michael reveals he's equally unsupported despite holding family together
jokes.explanation: Michael's rare moment of self-advocacy, delivered with characteristic understatement despite the deeper pain.

jokes.text: I let you drive, didn't I?
jokes.setup: Lucille thanking Michael for doing something nice
jokes.punchline: His gesture of love was letting her operate a deadly weapon
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy - treating potentially lethal enabling as an act of love. References her terrible driving record for added irony.

jokes.text: It's an idiot on a scooter at night? It's gotta be Gob.
jokes.setup: Seeing someone on a scooter
jokes.punchline: Immediately identifies GOB by his poor choices
jokes.explanation: Perfect maternal recognition - knowing your child by their bad judgment. The casual cruelty is perfectly Lucille.

jokes.text: I was thrown clear.
jokes.setup: Car accident with Michael concerned about frail mother
jokes.punchline: Lucille casually describes being ejected from vehicle
jokes.explanation: Casual delivery of dramatic physical trauma is perfectly Lucille. The matter-of-fact tone makes the absurd situation funnier.

jokes.text: Your mother asked me to pump you full of... Children's aspirin.
jokes.setup: Ominous phrasing about what Michael was given
jokes.punchline: It's just kid's aspirin
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection that makes something innocent sound sinister, then deflates. The pause adds to the effect.

jokes.text: I hardly felt it. In fact, actually, uh, I didn't feel it.
jokes.setup: Michael apologizing for the scooter accident
jokes.punchline: Victim admits he couldn't feel the impact, implying paralysis
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation from reassurance to alarming revelation. The delivery makes the horrible implication funnier.

jokes.text: It was a wild, wild ride.
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 asking about their kissing
jokes.punchline: Buster describes it like an amusement park attraction
jokes.explanation: Buster's awkward attempt at romance talk reveals his inexperience. The enthusiasm is endearingly inappropriate.

jokes.text: Totally cool. Didn't feel a thing. You know, Mom, I'm crazy about this aspirin. I can't believe we give it to children.
jokes.setup: Michael on children's aspirin after head injury
jokes.punchline: Loves the medication while being concerned about giving it to kids
jokes.explanation: Ironic commentary on medication safety from someone who's clearly impaired by it. Michael's earnestness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, and, uh, preferably French. I like the way they think.
jokes.setup: George Michael asking for fake passports
jokes.punchline: Wants to be French because he admires their worldview
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Michael naivety - treating fake identity as cultural exploration. The earnestness is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: She's an Olympian... hopeful. Swimming and some coaching. Yes, I will make the I.D.'s for you.
jokes.setup: Woman in GOB's bedroom during conversation
jokes.punchline: Awkward explanation that she's an 'athlete' who does 'coaching'
jokes.explanation: Typical GOB sleaziness with transparent euphemisms. Not particularly clever but consistent with character.

jokes.text: That's when Gob decided to take the yacht to South America for the summer.
jokes.setup: GOB learning Michael is incommunicado
jokes.punchline: Immediately plans elaborate yacht theft for vacation
jokes.explanation: Perfect GOB logic - any opportunity becomes a scheme. The casual way he escalates to international yacht theft is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I just jumped through a plate glass window on a date.
jokes.setup: Buster on phone with Dr. Miller
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of dramatic self-injury during romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster physical comedy delivered as casual conversation. The matter-of-fact tone makes the absurd action funnier.

jokes.text: Oh. Well, women love to laugh.
jokes.setup: Buster describing jumping through window
jokes.punchline: Doctor treats serious injury as comedy attempt
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dry response that treats self-harm as romantic strategy. The clinical delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Gob was just teaching me how to hit it with a hammer.
jokes.setup: Buster explaining his head injury
jokes.punchline: GOB was giving lessons in self-inflicted head trauma
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd explanation that makes perfect Bluth family sense. The casual delivery of insane content is gold.

jokes.text: It is obvious I'm not wearing a bra, right?
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to get attention in prison
jokes.punchline: Directly asks guard to notice her sexuality
jokes.explanation: Lindsay's desperate attempt to get the attention she expected. The directness makes it more cringe than clever.

jokes.text: Your mother snuck 'em in here stuffed in energy bar wrappers to keep me from getting strangled in the shower or worse.
jokes.setup: George Sr. explaining why he pays other inmates
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'protein bars' were actually gold smuggled for protection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to the protein bar setup. Dark prison humor with perfect AD-style reveal that recontextualizes earlier scenes.

jokes.text: But you keep coming back here, honey, and I'm going broke.
jokes.setup: George Sr. paying for Lindsay's protection
jokes.punchline: Her beauty is literally bankrupting him
jokes.explanation: Dark irony that Lindsay's vanity visits are financially ruining her father. Subverts her expectation of being harassed.

jokes.text: That's all I ever wanted from you, Daddy. For you to spend money on me.
jokes.setup: George Sr. revealing he's paying for her safety
jokes.punchline: Lindsay treats financial protection as emotional validation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay moment - turning genuine paternal sacrifice into materialistic satisfaction. Dark and perfectly character-specific.

jokes.text: But 'skiff' is appropriate, right?
jokes.setup: Just used nautical terminology
jokes.punchline: Seeks validation for his word choice
jokes.explanation: Classic GOB insecurity about appearing sophisticated. His need for approval about vocabulary is endearingly pathetic.

jokes.text: Which was behind the passenger seat. Which means that she picked you up and dragged you over to the driver's seat.
jokes.setup: Explaining how Michael got hit on back of head
jokes.punchline: Reveals Lucille's elaborate cover-up involving moving Michael's body
jokes.explanation: Good detective work that reveals the absurd lengths Lucille went to for her deception. Sets up the final confrontation.

jokes.text: Mom can't pick me up. She could barely pick up that bag of protein bars.
jokes.setup: GOB suggesting Lucille moved Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael references her supposed frailty with the heavy bag
jokes.explanation: Callback to the protein bars that sets up GOB's reveal about the Krugerrands. Michael's trust in his mother's frailty act.

jokes.text: Guess what? They weren't protein bars. They were gold Krugerrands. And Dad finally paid attention to my intellect.
jokes.setup: Mystery of the heavy protein bars
jokes.punchline: Reveals gold smuggling and Lindsay's pride in being smart for once
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to multiple setups with Lindsay's rare moment of intellectual pride. Recontextualizes several earlier scenes.

jokes.text: because we're brothers, Mom, and we kind of like each other.
jokes.setup: GOB explaining why he came back to help
jokes.punchline: Admits to minimal fraternal affection
jokes.explanation: Touching moment of brotherly affection delivered in the most understated way possible. Callbacks to earlier 'kind of like' exchange.

jokes.text: I'm a horrible mother.
jokes.setup: Caught in her deception
jokes.punchline: Returns to her earlier admission of being awful
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier self-awareness moment. Less funny the second time but shows some character growth.

jokes.text: That's crazy talk. You're a fantastic mother.
jokes.setup: Lucille admitting to being horrible
jokes.punchline: Sons immediately contradict obvious truth
jokes.explanation: Classic Bluth family dysfunction - enabling terrible behavior through denial. The automatic responses are perfectly coded.

jokes.text: You know what? I knew society would never accept this. And they never will.
jokes.setup: Theater refusing to let them see the movie
jokes.punchline: George Michael thinks it's about cousin relationships
jokes.explanation: Callback to narrator's framing of their relationship. George Michael's misunderstanding of the rejection is perfectly cringe.

jokes.text: I was definitely driving that time.
jokes.setup: Another car accident in the preview
jokes.punchline: Lucille admits fault for once
jokes.explanation: Callback to her driving problems with rare honesty. The casual admission makes her terrible driving record funnier.

jokes.text: I was definitely driving that time.
jokes.setup: Lucille in another car accident
jokes.punchline: She admits culpability this time
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows patterns repeating with slight variation

tmdb_overview: Michael loses his short-term memory, which Lucille uses to her advantage. Gob attempts to use the family yacht before Michael sells it. George Michael and Maeby attempt to see an R-rated movie about cousins having an affair. Buster and Lucille 2 take "the next step".
insight: Sustained 2.6 jokes-per-minute pace delivers 63 laughs on character absurdity and deadpan restraint.
